document.source_title: Tunisia validated by WHO as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem

claims.claim_text: Tunisia has eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.
claims.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Tunisia as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The organization that validated Tunisia's elimination of trachoma.
entities.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Tunisia as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

entities.name: Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus
entities.description: WHO Director-General who congratulated Tunisia on its achievement.
entities.evidence_text: “I congratulate Tunisia on this historic public health achievement,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.

entities.name: Dr. Hanan Balkhy
entities.description: WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean who commented on Tunisia's achievement.
entities.evidence_text: “I congratulate Tunisia on the elimination of trachoma as a public health problem,” said WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean Dr Hanan Balkhy.

entities.name: Dr. Mustapha Ferjani
entities.description: Minister of Health of Tunisia who spoke about the milestone.
entities.evidence_text: “Validation marks a historic achievement for Tunisia and reflects our country’s long-standing commitment to public health,” said Dr Mustapha Ferjani, Minister of Health of Tunisia.

entities.name: Dr. Ahmed Zouiten
entities.description: Acting WHO Representative in Tunisia who commented on the validation.
entities.evidence_text: “The validation of elimination of trachoma as a public health problem in Tunisia reflects the impact of sustained collaboration between the Ministry of Health and WHO,” said Dr Ahmed Zouiten.

events.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Tunisia as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

metrics.name: Countries validated for trachoma elimination
metrics.evidence_text: Tunisia becomes the 31st country in the world to be validated as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

citations.title: Tunisia validated by WHO as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem
citations.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Tunisia as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.

source_credibility.reason: The information is provided by the World Health Organization.

geolocation.name: Tunisia
geolocation.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Tunisia as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem.
